item.description:
a day running on 3 and a half hours of sleep isn't too bad honestly
but then i went to bed at 3:30 again ..
every time i play smash i think i can do better but then i just get cooked anyways lol.
fever dream of a day that didn't have too much happen

item.title: ice maps
item.description:
what a day. woke up went to chipotle. got a call from lockheed martin that they wanted to extend an offer while eating. skipped a class so that i could see some friends, ate dinner, then decided to skateboard despite the ice..
my skateboard has been thoroughly ran through and iced. i really want to get better at doing tricks.
through discussion with some of my colleagues i become more and more conflicted upon whether i should want to work in defense. it seems like a waste to give up opportunities working with some very bright people, but i suppose the work isn't very moral, and similarly bright people could be reached in other places. the only benefit is the amount of filters that are required to enter the industry, which i guess builds you a bit more of a moat compared to other industries.
i also feel attached to the idea of the united states of america. i am not satisfied with how america is at the current moment, and i should be doing more to fix it. regardless, i have an attachment to the idea of the US of A, and serving your country. im not really sure why i feel that kind of patriotism, because it has never really presented itself in other sectors of my life. maybe because of boy scouts? maybe because i am brainwashed by the government.
i wonder to what point i am willing to sell my morals.
10 PM - 3 AM finished digital design lab doing some stupid IC testing. 3 AM - 4:30 AM did 2k2 homework.
first semi all nighter of the semester

item.title: bush
item.description:
i was pushed into a bush
and then i walked 30 minutes back from chauncey so my toes were really cold again. but the vents saved me. no canes stop.
i honestly cannot remember anything else memorable about this day.

item.title: form over function
item.description:
my friend tried getting me into skating. he showed me a couple videos, mainly of flashy street skating. one could say that i was persuaded into it.
the search for skates themselves took a tad bit longer. in many aspects of my life, past a certain point of performance, i will always go for my preferred form over a slight benefit in function.
the only pair of frames i could find that i wanted were 500 dollars.
the dilemma continues.

item.title: the state of the cs major
item.description:
my friend (j) who is one year under me had a pretty long conversation with me the other week which first prompted my thinking upon this topic. it started pretty normally and progressed around the topic of cs majors being cooked.
it started from his seeking advice upon my route for getting to intern and work in tech, then developed through his feelings on the application process. strangely enough, i think i've had a similar conversation to most people with careers in this area this year.
i don't have enough time to write out all my advice, but heres a general frame you can follow (that i think will serve you well in most majors, not just cs, or compe, or stem)
1. try to get freshman experience as soon as you can. in my experience research or technical experience will both serve you well, myself, i did research and i think it may be a bit better of an option for freshman for a reason i'll discuss below (you could also just do both if you have that kinda time). if you're having a hard time finding a lab, you can cold email professors until one responds (at least one will, its a lot easier than getting an internship). just make yourself a template that glazes one of their recent projects, discusses what specifically they do that you are interested in, then focus on what you can provide to their research. technical club wise, at least for purdue, just putting in a lot is the best method to get a lot back.
2. after you have something technical on your resume, the place that i find it easiest to make real world connections (which is the most important portion of this process if you want to land a job quicker than others), is the career fair. PLEASE attend your career fair if you would like a job. it isn't every day that you are able to have a direct conversation with a recruiter just by investing some of your time. additionally, if you have any nepo connections, please please please leverage them. if you have any method, getting that help to put your foot in your door will make things infinitely easier for yourself in the future. my connections didn't really help me too directly in my year, but if they would have i definitely would have utilized them.
3. other than that, just cold email and spam apply. make sure your resume hits all the points in the required/preferred skill listings on the job. it helps get you through ats. if you can find a hiring manager or anyone with authority at the company (if its small) as a contact, send them a cold email or linked dm introducing yourself and your application.
4. my interests generally align more to startups, but i find that finding the human connection or that distinguishing point to get yourself in is a lot easier at smaller companies rather than the big faang fish. emailing startups with that cold message template you developed for research is definitely possible.
there's definitely a non zero chance that at the end of this process you weren't able to make any process towards a real job.
thats ok.
that's simply the way the industry is moving right now, and theres always next year for you to stack more and try again. at the very least. for summer, i would try and find a research position if you aren't able to land a full fledged internship. this is where that school year research comes in handy! if you've been working with a professor, good chance is they'll have work for you to be doing over the summer as well. since it's hard to land that first internship as a freshman, this is why i recommended research as a path vs. technical club earlier. generally though, even without any structure work like research or an internship, summer is an amazing time to build your own personal projects that address your interests, and make things such as open source contributions (which look great on a resume btw)
best of luck, feel free to reach out to me on discord at (jk.won) if you would ever like to talk deeper about anything (i dont even know if people read this blog)

item.title: nanimo
item.description:
there was this time in fifth grade where i had a really big crush on this girl. i don't think i knew if i had a crush on her at the time. but theres this really really really burnt in core memory that i have of us together.
i was reading naruto at the library, and she kept trying to talk to me. but for some reason (probably because i liked her) every time i would try to respond, my chest would tighten up and i felt my face turn red while sweat began to bead on my forehead.
so instead of talking to this girl that i liked i sat there and read like four volumes of naruto while basically ignoring her.
sometimes i wonder why i was so awkward, because that was the last time i ever saw that girl. we went off to different middle schools, high schools, universities. it really wouldn't have hurt me if i just talked to her. i wonder if anyone from my younger years really remembers me (that i wasn't friends with). i feel like i was too quiet to get too close to most of them.
i have so many stories that i remember, i feel like the first time i really gained any kind of conscious act was 3rd grade. i remember when i made a pokemon ttrpg and the time i found a shiny pidgey on a pokemon clone and the day in fourth grade when i found out how babies were made with r. i remember emailing my fourth grade teacher in eleventh grade and finding out that she remembered who i was, and was having a kidney transplant. i remember eating the dark chocolate we were supposed to be using for a science experiment in third grade and getting in trouble.
i really want to rewatch naruto .

item.title: short filmings
item.description:
i filmed some bits with my friend who helped me with a short film i wanted to make last year. unfortunately that film has not seen the light of day yet. it's a lot harder to translate your emotions into concrete scenes, than one might think.
also flog2 is actually the worst format to grade ever.
i imagine that all of us are born in log. with low contrast, with technical ceilings, with over or under exposure or maybe perfect exposure.
and as we live our lives, we're slowly seasoned into existence. of course, you could be touched in a way you might not want. a hue shift, a white balance. a result unpleasant to your overall image.
and maybe your image is tweaked too much, to the point where your data corrupts. but as long as you reset, you'll be back to the same base. with that being stated, i believe that nurture takes some precedence over nature. some fundamental traits can't be changed through grading (for example, the image itself, the framing and content), but with clever technical knowledge, an advanced colorist will be able to harness most shots just by consistently working with them.
i wonder what i look like graded. i think i was raised well. rec709 lut. layers and adjustments. maybe a couple of accidental splashes and tweaks here and there.

item.title: roller bladers
item.description:
sometimes when i write these little snippets i feel like im horsemeat in his car.
i went roller skating with friends after classes (friday night). it was really really really cold outside like if i went to sleep i probably would have died cold. i learned a new way to shemagh wrap my whole head so my ears don't freeze off. my carhartt jacket hood isn't really the right shape so i don't like to put it on.
theres a couple of friends that i've begun to see a lot these days, just a side effect of this new bubble of friends i've found. it's really different since my roommate left this semester. i didn't think much would change but surprisingly the whole dynamic has shifted.
one of them enjoys cherries. maraschino cherries. cherry flavored lollipops.
we walked the cherry enjoyer back and by the time i got home it was 4 am. i sat upon a vent for a couple minutes on the way back so my toes wouldn't freeze.
there's this movie called 100 meters, it's about running (not really its more about life). it reminded me of oysters with lemon. or maybe marty supreme. probably both.

item.title: sick day
item.description:
today felt like i had missed a day of school
and then everything i heard was missing a bit of context
it's hard not to get fomo on days like these
but theres not enough time in the world to make sure that you won't fear missing out on everything. so how should we rank what parts matter less and what matters more?

item.title: hold me said the dream
item.description: i dream of the smell of american households and the rubiks cube at the japanese school festival and the banana leaves they served food upon

item.title: me through your eyes
item.description:
i met someone new today (jms) when he came up to me he said hi i love your videos
i wonder what the aggregate impression that people have of me are
what has changed in the way that people approach me and the way i approach people based off of the fact that i've posted videos of myself before?
do i have to fit into the character i portray?
what if i don't fit into the character i write
i would like to make more content and branch out, but that would simply mean that the surface area of people that i reach would grow even further. i'm comfortable at a stage of tiny popularity/recognition that i have. but to grow more means more results and more that i achieve.
and the competitive spirit in me doesn't care much for my modesty either
i want to be bigger and better and doing more
haha i wonder how these writing blurbs change perception too

item.title: searching for the one
item.description:
surprisingly, or maybe not surprisingly, out of my group of closer friends, both from university and high school, there is a very small percentage who have sigificant others. in fact, it wouldn't be much of a reach to say that all of our bloodlines are not going to continue forwards if the current situation continues on for too long.
i think its scary to think about relationships in the big 26. theres so much negative rhetoric on the internet regarding either men/women, or even relationships in general.
it's a very easy way out to just say that you're working on yourself, you don't need to search or chase for love. but upon deeper investigation i think this is a thin veil meant to protect me (and whoever else uses this approach) from rejection (which is a huge fear of mine). thanks to my friends at syse across many syse conferences for helping me figure this one out.
despite what they say about building your garden and letting the butterflies come in, i think it's very necessary to be actually putting yourself out there. unfortunately, this is the real world, and you won't get some huzz by sitting at home and watching anime. sure, one or two of you might get really lucky, but for the most part, if you don't try to actively search for a relationship, or at least see the opportunity for a relationship in your day to day life, i think it will be more than easy to wake up one day single and 30.
when i observe my handful of non single friends, the relationships i see them have are all so unique. each one has their own routines, and inside jokes, but they all seem to have a deep bond, formed so that it becomes hard to imagine one without the other.
considering they started off as strangers at one point, it's difficult for me to imagine ever getting to know a stranger, then building up that rapport with them. then again, that's how i felt this summer before my internship, and by the end my coworkers really felt like family.
that being said, i wonder when it'll be that i find **her**. the one that will become my best friend and provide such wondrous companionship. believe it or not, relationships are not my forte so i'd place my bets that it might never happen, but theres always things to hope for. i'd much rather take it slow and find the special person who can make things feel right, than remain stifled in a relationship created for mutual benefit on no foundation.
i'm rather content with where i am now, i have work that excites me, lots of friends, and things to do that fill my time.
i wouldn't mind it if this is how things turn out, but i hope it isn't due to lack of trying.

item.title: oysters with a squeeze of lemon
item.description:
have you ever been the best at something?
i don't think ive ever been so good at something that one could look at me and consider me a master at my craft.
for someone with so many different interests ive never been able to break through that upper ceiling where i embody something desperately out of passion.
and what i think is difficult is that you can't have passion taught to you. to obsess over something is not something that can be given, to be gifted to another person.
if i told you that i loved the flavor of lime ice cream, but you didn't like lime ice cream, i couldn't explain to you how to make yourself enjoy that flavor. i think the most one can do is lead you down the path that MIGHT lead to obsession. you lead the horse to water to see if they want to drink.
the first time i had salmiakki was in a digital electronics class. its a salty spicy bitter licourice that people either love or don't love i think. but after an initial reaction of disgust, i tried a piece every day for that week and eventually i was hooked.
a taste i've never grown to love is oysters. when i see other people that eat oysters, i see how they relish in every bite. then, i feel the urge, the inspiration to try an oyster. maybe with a squeeze of lemon sprinkled over the bunch. but the moment the oyster touches my tongue, i realize, wow i dislike this flavor.
and no matter how much someone who enjoys the oyster might try and convince me otherwise, i will never love the flavor of oysters. i won't be able to tell you why, because it's simply the way it is.
i see those that have mastered one thing able to achieve more than an ordinary person can. i wonder if the taste of reaching such a level of success brings them a similar obsession to other activites. like if they make it once, they know they have that capability in them, allowing them to reach through that upper ceiling faster.
i think what i fear most is that the most i'll ever amount to is someone who was obsessed with the idea of obsession. like someone infatuated with the idea of love, with the idea of having the perfect romance. a housewife that secretly reads romance novels while her deadbeat husband goes to work and she has dropped the kids off at school.
theres many domains where i want that obsession and i hope that if i ever manage to reach that status, it'll slowly infect the others. but first, i suppose that i will have to reach that status first.
i think that having a balance in your life is good. but i think that balance is what you gain after you ascend past a certain point. i think most people that have ever been great say the same thing with different words. to reach the top of their craft, there had to be a time in their life where nothing else mattered.
thankfully, i think i'm someone who at the very least knows the flavors they like. it's simply that i have trouble defining what i would like to spend my time eating.
but after a year of taking a bite out of everything here and there, i think it's easy to identify that my time would be better off spent gorging on one flavor to the point where being in that position of "balance" is enough to maintain it. then and only then should i move on to the next taste.

item.title: justin the fisherman and sushi in the midwest
item.description:
got lunch with a good friend that i havent seen in a while. decent katsu curry and mediocre california rolls. sushi in the midwest !
peach oolong tea at vienna
frosted lemondae at chickfila
i watched josee (the korean live action version of josee the tiger and the fish) with my friend (mk) and my new roommate (jp)
quite the experience and a slightly confusing movie, probably going to spin back and watch more variations/this specific film again. i actually haven't watched any of the other versions of josee the tiger and the fish before, only seen snippets of it through assorted other forms of media.
am i a qualified person to give career advice to my friends ?

item.title: born to have a side part
item.description:
my friend (m) ate a giant p terrys sandwich for lunch 1700 calories of bulk
after my hair gets wet it dries to the side. if i didnt make it go to the right as well, it would all go to the left.
i ate dinner (mapo tofu) that my friends cooked and met a new person today, a very nice experience. i need to do it more.
the rainbow sherbet wasnt sour and this upset me
and then played smash bros (dk and mc) and drank banana milk after somehow greninja gets me my best results so far

item.title: wanna log
item.description:
i think it'd be pretty cool to make a log for every day of the year from now on
i have some other goals besides that for this year - im gonna bench 225 by the end of the year my current 1 rep max is 195 200 ish but i havent been at that strength since november ish. i went to the gym today and i got 2 reps of 165, i could probably do 1 of 185 right now. - i would like to be able to run a half marathon before the year ends. but theres always ice outside and i dont like running indoors which sucks. - lets hit 100k on socials. just for the fun and games. - hopefully i will write a book. or at least a very large portion of it.
i recently found out that theres a good chance i have a job this summer so thats good news for me! and also it is lowkey my dream summer opportunity ,,,
today was a boring day, i got the verbal offer for the job yesterday afternoon. todays highlights were a not amazing gym session which was supposed to be legs but turned into upper because the one friend who wanted to hit legs came an hour late.
